{{Short description|Quantitative property transferred to perform work or heat}}
{{Infobox physical quantity
| name = Energy
| image = Lightning over Oradea Romania cropped.jpg
| caption = [[Lightning]] is a dramatic natural display of energy transfer.
| symbols = <math>E</math>
| SI_unit = [[joule]] (J = kg·m²/s²)
| conserved = Yes
}}

'''Energy''' is the [[physical quantity|quantitative property]] that must be transferred to an object in order to perform [[Work (physics)|work]] on, or to [[heat]], the object. Energy is a [[Conservation of energy|conserved]] quantity; the law of [[conservation of energy]] states that energy can be [[energy transformation|converted]] in form, but not created or destroyed.

The [[International System of Units|SI]] unit of energy is the [[joule]] (J), defined as 1 kg·m²/s². Common units include the [[calorie]], the [[kilowatt-hour]] (kWh), and the [[electronvolt]] (eV).

== Forms of energy ==
Energy exists in many forms:

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Form !! Description
|-
| [[Kinetic energy]] || Energy of motion: <math>K = \frac{1}{2}mv^2</math>
|-
| [[Potential energy]] || Stored energy due to position or configuration
|-
| [[Thermal energy]] || Energy associated with random molecular motion
|-
| [[Chemical energy]] || Energy stored in chemical bonds
|-
| [[Electrical energy]] || Energy from electric charges and fields
|-
| [[Nuclear energy]] || Energy from nuclear binding and reactions
|-
| [[Radiant energy]] || Energy carried by electromagnetic waves
|-
| [[Rest energy]] || <math>E_0 = mc^2</math> — mass–energy equivalence
|}

== Kinetic energy ==
{{Main|Kinetic energy}}
The kinetic energy of a particle of mass <math>m</math> moving at speed <math>v</math> is:
:<math>K = \frac{1}{2}mv^2</math>

The relativistic expression is <math>K = (\gamma - 1)mc^2</math>.

== Potential energy ==
{{Main|Potential energy}}
Potential energy <math>U</math> is stored energy associated with conservative forces; <math>\mathbf{F} = -\nabla U</math>. Common types:
* '''Gravitational potential energy''': <math>U_g = mgh</math> (near Earth's surface)
* '''Elastic potential energy''': <math>U_s = \frac{1}{2}kx^2</math> (spring)
* '''Electrostatic potential energy''': <math>U_e = k_e\frac{q_1 q_2}{r}</math>

== Conservation of energy ==
{{Main|Conservation of energy}}
The [[first law of thermodynamics]] and the principle of conservation of mechanical energy state that for an isolated system, total energy is constant:
:<math>E = K + U + U_{\text{int}} = \text{constant}</math>

Noether's theorem links energy conservation to [[time translation symmetry]].

== Work and heat ==
Energy transfer occurs via:
* '''[[Work (physics)|Work]]''': <math>W = \int \mathbf{F} \cdot d\mathbf{r}</math> — organized energy transfer
* '''[[Heat]]''': <math>Q</math> — disorganized energy transfer due to temperature difference

The work–energy theorem: <math>W_{\text{net}} = \Delta K</math>

== Power ==
{{Main|Power (physics)}}
[[Power (physics)|Power]] is the rate of energy transfer:
:<math>P = \frac{dE}{dt}</math>
The SI unit is the [[watt]] (W = J/s).

== Energy in special relativity ==
{{Main|Mass–energy equivalence}}
Einstein's <math>E = mc^2</math> unifies mass and energy. The [[energy–momentum relation]]:
:<math>E^2 = (pc)^2 + (mc^2)^2</math>

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Thermodynamics]]
* [[Exergy]]
* [[Renewable energy]]
* [[Energy density]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Energy| ]]
[[Category:Physics]]
[[Category:Conservation laws]]
